quests.title: Netherite Monstrosity
quests.subtitle: It's made of Netherite. Good luck.
description: The Netherite Monstrosity is your introduction to the Cataclysm boss line.
You'll find it lurking in massive Nether structures — it is large, armored, and not happy to see you.
Drops: guaranteed Resource Catalyst, and occasionally rare Cataclysm-specific loot.
Recommended loadout: Refined Obsidian armor (full set), a strong sword or axe with high-tier enchantments, and plenty of healing.
If you die, respawn, restock, try again. This is supposed to be hard.

quests.title: Ignis — Lord of Fire
quests.subtitle: Apex Boss I. Fire Resistance is not optional.
description: Ignis is the first of three Apex Bosses — the hardest enemies in the modpack.
It inhabits volcanic biomes and uses fire-based attacks. Without Fire Resistance, you will die to the environment alone.
Drops: Mythic Catalyst (1, guaranteed) and Singularity Shards (1-2).
Mythic Catalysts are required for Tier 4 MA seeds. Singularity Shards are needed for the Fusion Reactor Controller.
Recommended: Fire Resistance potions, Totem of Undying, Refined Obsidian armor with Protection IV, a Mekanism Atomic Alloy-tier weapon.

quests.title: The Harbinger and Ender Guardian
quests.subtitle: Apex Bosses II and III. The End is not safe.
description: The Harbinger and the Ender Guardian are both found in End-dimension structures.
The Harbinger is fast, aggressive, and uses teleportation attacks. The Ender Guardian commands the void.
Both drop Singularity Shards — you need multiple for the Fusion Reactor Controller.
Together with Ignis, defeating all three Apex Bosses gives you the materials for every Tier 5 recipe.
Tip: Set up a Waystone in the End before engaging. You'll want a fast way back to your base.

quests.title: Mythic Catalyst: Automated
quests.subtitle: Boss drops, without the boss fight. Eventually.
description: Once you've killed an Apex Boss and obtained their Mob Effigy, you can place it near a Drygmy.
The Drygmy will then generate Mythic Catalysts passively — no more boss runs required.
This is the only way to fully automate Mythic Catalyst production.
You'll need a steady supply for Tier 4 MA seeds and any other recipes using them.
Setup: Drygmy Farm (from T4) + Cataclysm Boss Effigy adjacent = automated Mythic Catalysts.

quests.title: Tier 4 Seeds — Mekanism Metals
quests.subtitle: Osmium from dirt. The mining industry is dead.
description: Mystical Agradditions Tier 4 seeds produce Mekanism metals: Osmium, Tin, Lead, Uranium, and Fluorite.
Each seed recipe requires a Mythic Catalyst — obtained from Cataclysm Apex Bosses or your Drygmy farm.
Once Osmium Seeds are running, your Mekanism supply chain becomes self-sustaining.
Uranium Seeds provide passive Uranium for Mekanism Generators, and Fluorite for the Electrolytic Separator.
Plant on Essence Farmland for the best growth rate. Automate harvesting with Create for maximum throughput.

quests.title: Digital Miner
quests.subtitle: Point it at the ground. Watch the ore arrive.
description: The Mekanism Digital Miner automatically scans a configurable area and mines specific blocks.
Set a filter for any ore or block type and it will systematically strip-mine the entire radius without you lifting a finger.
Recipe note: Requires the Mechanical Crafter with Refined Obsidian and Precision Mechanisms.
Power requirement: significant FE/t — your Alternator + Solar setup should be upgraded before running this.
Place it, set the filter, give it power and a Silk Touch-equivalent upgrade, and walk away.

quests.title: MEGA Cells — Maximum Capacity
quests.subtitle: 64 million items. Zero excuses for a messy base.
description: The 64M MEGA Cell is the largest storage unit in the pack. One cell. 64 million items. You probably don't need more.
Applied Mekanistics adds Mekanism Chemical Cells for your AE2 network — store Oxygen, Hydrogen, and other gases directly in ME Drives.
With MEGA Cells + Applied Mekanistics, your AE2 network can manage your entire material flow — items and chemicals — in one place.
This is the storage endgame. Build it, fill it, and never think about storage again.

quests.title: Fusion Reactor
quests.subtitle: Unlimited power. No, really.
description: The Mekanism Fusion Reactor is the most powerful energy source in the modpack.
Once running, it produces so much FE/t that every other generator becomes completely obsolete.
The Controller recipe is heavily gated — it requires:
• Singularity Shards — only obtainable from Cataclysm Apex Bosses (Ignis, Harbinger, Ender Guardian)
• Atomic Alloy — top-tier Mekanism alloy
• Refined Obsidian Ingots — Mekanism's hardest material
• AE2 ME Controller — requires Precision Mechanisms
Building the full reactor structure requires additional Fusion Reactor Frame blocks and a reactor port.
Fuel: D-T Fuel (Deuterium + Tritium, both from water in the Electrolytic Separator).
Once ignited, it never runs out of fuel as long as you supply water. This is the energy endgame.

quests.title: Gaia Awakens
quests.subtitle: You built it all. From a stick and two planks.
description: You started with nothing. You punched a tree. You built a Crafting Table.
Now you have a Fusion Reactor, a fully automated AE2 network, Mystical Agriculture growing every metal in the game, and Cataclysm bosses farming themselves via Drygmys.
The main progression of Gaia Awakening is complete.
But the world is still out there: Ars Nouveau spell mastery, Occultism rituals, Alex's Mobs and Caves to explore, Steam 'n' Rails to build, Apotheosis pinnacles to reach.
The end of the quest line is not the end of the game.
It never is.
